evals.prompt: Here is my manuscript on declining birth rates and management strategies of private universities in Turkey. Can you do a full peer review of it before I submit?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-paper-reviewer in full mode: runs Phase 0 field analysis, presents a 5-reviewer configuration card (EIC + 3 peer reviewers + Devil's Advocate) for confirmation, then produces 5 independent review reports plus an Editorial Decision Letter and prioritized Revision Roadmap.

assertions.value: Configures a 5-reviewer panel (Editor-in-Chief + 3 peer reviewers + Devil's Advocate) and produces multiple independent reviews culminating in a single Editorial Decision and a prioritized Revision Roadmap.

evals.prompt: I don't need a whole review, just help me check the methodology and statistical validity of this study's sampling and analysis.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-paper-reviewer in methodology-focus mode: activates field_analyst plus methodology_reviewer only, producing an in-depth methodology review covering research design, sampling strategy, statistical validity, effect sizes, and reproducibility.

assertions.value: Runs only the field_analyst and methodology_reviewer agents (not the full panel) and assesses sampling, statistical validity, effect sizes, and reproducibility.

evals.prompt: I revised my paper based on the previous round's comments and attached my response-to-reviewers. Can you do a verification review to confirm the revisions actually addressed each comment?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-paper-reviewer in re-review mode: builds a checklist from the original Revision Roadmap, EIC checks each item with FULLY_ADDRESSED / PARTIALLY_ADDRESSED / NOT_ADDRESSED / MADE_WORSE status, scans for new issues introduced during revision, and issues a new Decision.

assertions.value: Builds a revision-response checklist and marks each prior comment FULLY_ADDRESSED / PARTIALLY_ADDRESSED / NOT_ADDRESSED / MADE_WORSE, then issues a new Decision.

evals.prompt: Critique this paper hard - I want someone to attack the core argument, find the logical fallacies, and tell me the strongest counter-argument against my thesis.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-paper-reviewer (full review including the Devil's Advocate reviewer): surfaces the strongest counter-argument, detects cherry-picking, confirmation bias, overgeneralization, and logic-chain gaps, and flags any CRITICAL findings that block an Accept decision.

assertions.value: Engages the Devil's Advocate reviewer to produce the strongest counter-argument and detect logical fallacies; any CRITICAL finding blocks an Accept decision.

evals.prompt: Walk me through the problems in my paper using a guided approach so I understand them myself rather than just getting a list of fixes.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-paper-reviewer in guided mode: runs the reviews internally then enters Socratic dialogue, opening with strengths, progressively revealing methodology, domain, perspective, and Devil's Advocate issues through questions, and ending with a structured Revision Roadmap.

assertions.value: Enters a Socratic dialogue that opens with strengths and reveals issues through questions rather than dumping a fix list, ending with a structured Revision Roadmap.

evals.prompt: Help me draft the introduction and literature review sections for a new paper on AI in higher education quality assurance.
evals.expected_output: defers to alterlab-paper-writer / does not invoke alterlab-paper-reviewer (this is paper writing, not reviewing an existing manuscript)

evals.prompt: Before I write anything, do a deep literature investigation on how machine learning has been used to predict university closure risk.
evals.expected_output: defers to alterlab-deep-research / does not invoke alterlab-paper-reviewer (this is upstream topic investigation, not peer review of a manuscript)

evals.prompt: I already have the reviewer comments and the revision roadmap - now just make the actual edits to my manuscript for me.
evals.expected_output: defers to alterlab-paper-writer (revision mode) / does not invoke alterlab-paper-reviewer (the user wants the revisions executed, not the paper reviewed again)

evals.prompt: A journal asked me to be a reviewer. Write me one structured, checklist-based referee report on this manuscript with a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: defers to alterlab-peer-review / does not invoke alterlab-paper-reviewer (the user wants a single reviewer's structured report, not a simulated multi-reviewer panel with an editorial decision)
